zh.Clitheroe, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾可利黑洛
zh.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley'是Jordan de Mitton和Wymarka de Eland的兒子，於1210年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾可利黑洛。他和Anota de Clitherow至少有2個兒子。他去世在他的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mitton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from any of the places in Lancashire, Yorkshire, and the West Midlands, so named in Old English as ‘farmstead at a river confluence’, from (ge)m̄ȳthe ‘river confluence’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Mitten
meanings: English (Sussex):
variant of   Mitton  . The English surname in this spelling has been established in Ireland (Wexford) since the early 17th century.
topographic name from Middle English midde + tune, for a dweller in the ‘middle of the village’.

variants.name: Mittan
meanings: English: variant of   Mitton  .

variants.name: Mutton
meanings: English (Cornwall and Northamptonshire):
nickname from Middle English motoun ‘sheep’ (Old French mouton).
variant of   Mitton  .

nameMeanings.name: Bayley'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bailey
meanings: English: status name for a steward or official, from Middle English bailli ‘manager, administrator’ (Old French baillis, from Late Latin baiulivus, an adjectival derivative of baiulus ‘attendant, carrier, porter’).
English: habitational name from Bailey in Little Mitton, Lancashire, named with Old English beg ‘berry’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

English: occasionally a topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, from Middle English (Old French) bailli ‘outer courtyard of a castle’ (Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’, a derivative of bailer ‘to enclose’). This term became a placename in its own right, denoting a district beside a fortification or wall, as in the case of the Old Bailey in London, which formed part of the early medieval outer wall of the city.

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

en.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley' was born in 1210, in Clitheroe, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Jordan de Mitton and Wymarka de Eland. He had at least 2 sons with Anota de Clitherow. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley' est né en 1210, à Clitheroe, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Jordan de Mitton et Wymarka d’Eland. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Anota de Clitherow. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley' wurde 1210 in Clitheroe, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Jordan de Mitton und Wymarka de Eland geboren. Er hatte mit Anota de Clitherow mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley' è nato il nel 1210 nel Clitheroe, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Jordan de Mitton e Wymarka de Eland. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Anota de Clitherow. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Clitheroe, Lancashire, England: イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアクリスエロー
ja.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley'は1210年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアクリスエローでJordan de MittonとWymarka de Elandの息子として生まれた。彼はAnota de Clitherowとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley'(은)는 1210년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 클리더에서 Jordan de Mitton(와)과 Wymarka de Eland의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Anota de Clitherow(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

mn.Clitheroe, Lancashire, England: Clitheroe, Lancashire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley' nasceu em 1210, em Clitheroe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Jordan de Mitton e Wymarka de Eland. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Anota de Clitherow. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley' родился в 1210 г. в местности клитиро, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Jordan de Mitton и Wymarka de Eland. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Anota de Clitherow. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Otto de Mitton 'de Bayley' nació en 1210, en Clitheroe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Jordan de Mitton y Wymarka de Eland. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Anota de Clitherow. Murió en su pueblo.
